body.u: I decided to stay on at school at the age of 16, I took a Business studies course, I wanted to leave at 16 but the course was not available to me until I’d finished in the 5th year. The reason I wanted to leave at 16 was, I wanted to get a job and save up.

I was engaged at 16 and my boyfriend Ian was saving and had been for nearly three years. I wanted to help him so that we could get a good place to live. We wouldn’t get married until we had a decent place.
When I did eventually leave at 18 I started work, as a secretary, Ian wanted me to get a job in one of the londonLondon Transport offices, that was where he worked.
When we finalyfinally had enough to get a decent flat in Wimbledon we got married and I moved jobs, I did work at London Transport just as he wanted.
We carried on saving for two and a half years, and I fell pregnant with our first baby, Natalie. Our second child came along three years later, we called him Chris.
It was a struggle for four or five years, as I couldn’t go to work but we managed. It was a nice break from working anyway.
My parents died when I was about thirty my father died first. I used to think I couldn’t face life if Ian died first, but you have to, you’ve still got a few responsibilities when youryou're old.
I was very upset when my mother died we were always so close, but she was getting on so I was quite prepared for it.

Not much happened after that for about five years, then I had an unexpected visitor, a baby which we called Maria. I was pleased because the other two were now almost grown up and all the pleasures of them being toddlers were still in mind.
My first child got married at the age of 19 I was about 39 to 40 old then.
I was beginingbeginning to feel old, 39 ½ sounded a lot younger than 40.
The years passed quicker after that and by the age of 52 all my children had left home and Ian eventually died and left me on my own.
I did OK though, Ian was insured and I still had my job not for long though, then I had to make do with my pension and the Insurance money.

I was scared at nights after all the years of having somebody with me, also I got wary of young people, some were ok but others, well you heard so many stories of muggins and things, the last five years have been lonely, but my children and grandchildren, of which I have 10, still visit me. I suppose I’ll eventually go into a home for the rest of my days. But until then I’ll manage.
